prompt: Review a plan to add bulk dataset export API with security and safety gaps

assertions.text: Identifies SQL injection risk and references the existing quoteIdentifier() utility
Identifies cross-project data exfiltration as a distinct issue (dataset IDs not validated against projectId)
Identifies that archiver is only a transitive dependency (via exceljs or similar), not direct
Identifies file-backed repository fallback — if no Postgres, SELECT * FROM table won't work
Flags GET method as wrong and calculates or mentions URL length with UUID-sized IDs
Proposes reading original files from disk instead of re-querying Postgres, with specific benefits listed
Mentions COPY TO STDOUT CSV as a Postgres-native alternative to SELECT + json2csv
Identifies partial failure handling — what happens when headers are already sent (200) but a dataset fails mid-stream
Specifies required response headers (Content-Type, Content-Disposition, or Transfer-Encoding)
Proposes a concrete alternative plan or revised implementation approach (not just 'fix these issues')
References at least 3 specific line numbers from the actual codebase
